name: Erlang's Third Eye
description: Erlang Shen's Third Eye, also known as the Eye of Heaven, is a legendary feature of the Chinese god Erlang Shen, a powerful and righteous deity often depicted as a warrior. This divine eye is located in the center of his forehead and is not merely a physical attribute but a source of immense power and profound insight. The Third Eye grants Erlang Shen the ability to see through deception, perceive truth hidden from ordinary eyes, and discern the nature of spirits and demons. It allows him to penetrate illusions and uncover hidden dangers, making him an unparalleled warrior and protector. Moreover, the Third Eye possesses the power to unleash destructive beams of light, capable of vanquishing evil and obliterating obstacles in his path. Its power is not limited to combat; it also symbolizes divine judgment and the ability to see the world with clarity and impartiality. In many stories, Erlang Shen uses his Third Eye to overcome challenges, defeat formidable foes, and maintain order in the celestial and mortal realms. It represents his unwavering commitment to justice and his unparalleled ability to see through the facades of the world, ensuring that truth prevails. This powerful eye is a testament to Erlang Shen's divine status and unwavering dedication to upholding righteousness.
subtitle: Divine insight and destructive power.
origin: Born with Erlang Shen as a divine attribute.

keyMyths.title: Journey to the West Encounter
keyMyths.description: During Sun Wukong's rebellion, Erlang Shen's Third Eye allowed him to see through Wukong's disguises and ultimately subdue the Monkey King.

keyMyths.title: Clearing the Peach Mountain
keyMyths.description: Erlang Shen used his Third Eye to split Peach Mountain and release his mother, symbolizing his filial piety and divine power.

sources.author: Anthony C. Yu
sources.description: Translation and commentary on the classic novel.
sources.text: Translation and commentary on the classic novel.

sources.author: Lihui Yang
